questions.scenario: The production project is linked to a shared trial billing account. The resources work, but finance cannot explain the charge owner. What should the review fix?

options.text: Link the project to the correct production billing account through an approved billing change.
Rename the Cloud Run service, because service names decide who pays.
Delete labels, because labels make billing impossible.
Move the region, because regions are billing accounts.

questions.explanation: The project owns resources, but the linked billing account pays for usage. Billing linkage needs explicit ownership.

questions.scenario: A Cloud SQL task fails because the Cloud SQL Admin API is not enabled in production. The same task worked in dev. What does the team learn?

options.text: API enablement belongs to the project, so each project needs its own service readiness check.
Cloud SQL cannot be used with Cloud Run.
The billing account has become a database.
The region must be global for every database task.

questions.explanation: Enabled APIs are part of project setup. A working dev project does not prove production has the same enabled services.

questions.scenario: Before the first production deploy, which checks belong together?

options.text: The project is linked to the intended active billing account.
Needed APIs are enabled in the production project.
Relevant project or regional quotas are available for the first release.
The display name sounds official enough to skip billing review.

questions.explanation: Billing, APIs, and quotas are separate setup facts. A display name is not enough evidence.
